{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gose, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[Gose, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped stone coffins, made by cutting and assembling [[tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped stone coffin can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Gose, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[Gose, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped stone coffins, made by cutting and assembling [[tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped stone coffin can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

"Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452994]] Wikidata name: Muro Miya-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.442839|N|135.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452994]] Wikidata name: Muro Miya-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.442839|N|135.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452994]] Wikidata name: Muro Miya-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.442839|N|135.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of [[:en:Silla|Silla]] and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary [[:en:Emperor Kōan|Emperor Kōan]], whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of [[:en:Kintetsu Gose Station|Kintetsu Gose Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Gose Line|Gose Line]].
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Muro Miya-yama Kofun]][[en:Muro Miyayama Kofun]][[ja:室宮山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452994]] Wikidata name: Muro Miya-yama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Muro Miyayama Kofun | native_name =室宮山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = 750302 Muro Miyayama Kofun aerial.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Muro Miyayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.442839|N|135.733333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|26|34.23|N|135|44|0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Gose, Nara|Gose, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken [[:en:Granite|Granite]] stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. {{nihongo|''' Muro Miyayama Kofun'''|室宮山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Muro neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2022 and again in 2024.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1937|title=室宮山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> The tumulus is also called the {{nihongo|'''Muro-no-Ohaka'''|室大墓|}}. It is the largest tumulus in the Katsuragi region and the 18th largest in Japan, and is estimated to have been built in the early 5th century.
==Overview== Muro Miyayama Kofun is located on the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. It was built facing west, using the tip of a ridge at the northern foot of the Kose mountain range, in the center of Gose city. It is a large {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 228 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the west-southwest. The mound is constructed in three tiers, the outside of which is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' consisting of broken {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} stones, and each tier surrounded by cylindrical and morning glory-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures. The ''haniwa'' at the top of the circular portion are figurative ''haniwa'' in the form of houses and granaries. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/413162|title=室宮山古墳 形象埴輪|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> A shield-shaped moat surrounds the mound, and a ''baizuka'' secondary mound, the {{nihongo|Nekozuka Kofun|ネコ塚古墳||}}, is built on the surrounding bank. A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama". "Muro" is an ancient place name, and it appears in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' as "Muro-go" in Katsuragi County, {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 105 meters and height of 2 meters and the rectangular anterior portion is 110 meters wide and 22 meters high.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Several [[archaeological excavation]]s have been carried out, revealing that there are an estimated six burial facilities in total, two in the circular posterior portion, part, two in the rectangular anterior portion, and one each in the two the protruding parts. The two burials in the rear mound are vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s containing a chest-shaped Sarcophaguss, made by cutting and assembling {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} slabs, with the long side stones standing on top of the bottom stone and the short side stones fitted in front and behind, and the inside was painted vermilion. In the southern burial chamber, the chest-shaped Sarcophagus can be viewed on-site. This long-chest-shaped sarcophagus, also known as the "Great King's Coffin," has a 3.77 meter long lid with eight tortoise-shell decorations on the top, and six rope-hanging protrusions. The {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were in pieces due to tomb-robbing, but many items have been excavated, including a triangular-rimmed {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with divine beasts, armor, iron swords, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads. A similar sarcophagus was found in the north burial chamber, and boat-shaped pottery was one of the notable grave goods. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The construction of this tumulus is estimated to have been around the beginning of the 5th century, during the middle of the Kofun period, with the tumulus most likely the grave of Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko, who is mentioned in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as a military commander of Japanese forces on the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]] in the war against the Kingdom of {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} and the ancestor of the Katsuragi clan. His daughter was empress during the time of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. There is an alternative theory that this is the tomb of the semi-legendary {{ill|Emperor Kōan|en|Emperor Kōan|WD=Q312821|lt=Emperor Kōan}}, whose palace (according to the ''Kojiki'') was located in this area.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about 3.3 kilometers south of {{ill|Kintetsu Gose Station|en|Kintetsu Gose Station|WD=Q11639107|lt=Kintetsu Gose Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Gose Line|en|Gose Line|WD=Q4792932|lt=Gose Line}}.
<gallery> 室宮山古墳 復原図.png|Outline drawing of Muro Miyayama Kofun showing locations of burials Muro Miya-yama Kofun minami-sekishitsu-2.jpg|Entrane to south burial chamber in posterior mound 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-1.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 家形埴輪-2.JPG|House-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳出土 衝角付冑・盾形埴輪.JPG|Armor-shaped haniwa 室宮山古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

A [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] has been enshrined on the posterior circular mound since ancient times, hence the name "Miyayama".
